item.author: rockroman

item.title: 改进现有的应用程序！ | Improving existing apps !
item.description:
发表于 4 月 30 日

大家好 ！ 我叫ziadOUA，最近开始学习android开发。

情况

我使用了很多应用程序。 我喜欢它们漂亮，遵循 Material 3 指南，并具有动态主题。 但是，我很少遇到任何将它们重新组合在一起的应用程序。 所以我决定简单地分叉我喜欢的应用程序的功能，并根据我的喜好调整它们。

怎么做

分叉你最喜欢的应用程序然后修改它们是相当简单的。
以下是如何开始：

例子

我需要 Google Wallet 的替代品（我不喜欢将关键数据存储在 Google 应用程序中这一事实），所以我下载了一个很好的替代品： Catima 。
但我发现设计中的某些元素未经修饰，因此我基于 Catima 构建了自己的替代方案，称为zCard 。

截图

一旦暂停，ziadoua 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ziadoua 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ziadoua 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ziadoua 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 ziadoua 的方法：

降低 ziadoua 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 ziadoua 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ziad OUALHADJ

Posted on Apr 30

Hello everybody ! My name is ziadOUA, and I recently started learning android development.

The situation

I use a lot of apps. And I like them to be beautiful, to follow the material 3 guidelines, and to feature dynamic theming. But, I rarely encounter any app that reunites them all. So I decided to simply fork the apps I liked for its functionality, and tweak them to my liking.

How to do it

Forking your favorite apps to then modify them is fairly simple.
Here is how to get started :

Example

I needed an alternative to Google Wallet (I didn't like the fact that I would store critical data in a Google app), so I downloaded a good alternative : Catima.
But I found some elements of the design unpolished, so I built my own alternative, based on Catima, called zCard.

Screenshots

Once suspended, ziadoua will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ziadoua will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ziadoua will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ziadoua is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ziadoua:

Make all posts by ziadoua less visible

ziadoua consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ziadoua will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ziadoua

item.title: 使用 Npm Workspaces 和 Lerna 设置 Monorepo | Monorepo setup with Npm Workspaces & Lerna
item.description:
在本文中，我们将看到如何使用 Lerna 和 Npm 工作空间设置一个单一仓库。

如果您有兴趣使用Vite、Pnpm 和 Typescript设置单一存储库，您可以在此处查看我之前的博客。

为什么要使用 Lerna？

要用好 monorepos，你需要一个好的 monorepo 工具，而 Lerna 正是这样。 在这种情况下，Lerna 可以通过管理不同包中的任务、版本控制、代码部署等繁重工作来拯救我们。

在多个包中运行任务：Lerna 为我们提供了一个开箱即用的解决方案，可以使用lerna run命令在多个包中一起运行任务。 例如，假设你的 monorepo 中有 3 个包，你想为所有这些包运行test脚本，使用 Lerna，你只需从项目的根目录运行npx lerna run test命令，它就会运行每个包内的test脚本（您必须分别在 package.json 中为所有项目定义test脚本才能使其正常工作）。

  • 可视化工作区：当工作区变得非常大时，新开发人员通常会难以理解工作区的层次结构以及理解不同包如何链接在一起。 只需运行npx nx graph ，它就会在你的浏览器上启动一个项目可视化图。

    • 您可以在此处查看 Lerna 功能的完整列表。

    谁使用 Lerna？

    笑话

  • 创建 React 应用程序

  • 嵌套

    • 项目设置

    先决条件

    要继续学习，您必须在系统中安装Node ，我假设您已经安装了。 除此之外，Javascript/Typescript 和 React 的一些基本知识将有助于理解我们将要编写的代码。

    项目概况

    就本文而言，我们的 monorepo 将有两个包：

    1. 入门

      让我们从在您想要的位置创建一个新目录开始。

      然后，移动到新创建的目录中并初始化 Lerna 工作区。

       # npm-lerna-monorepo npx lerna init

      此命令将执行以下操作：

      • 创建一个package.json文件（如果它不存在）并将lerna添加为开发依赖项。

      • 创建并配置一个新的lerna.json文件。

      • 在package.json中配置 npm 工作区。

      • 创建一个packages目录，其中将包含我们所有的 monorepo 包。

       lerna.json .gitignore package.json packages

      现在，为了在我们的项目中使用 Typescript，我们需要在根级别安装一些依赖项。

       npm i typescript @types/node -D

      安装上述依赖项后，创建一个通用的tsconfig.json文件，该文件将由我们应用程序的所有其他包扩展。

       { "compilerOptions": { "module": "commonjs", "declaration": false, "noImplicitAny": false, "removeComments": true, "noLib": false, "emitDecoratorMetadata": true, "experimentalDecorators": true, "target": "es6", "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true, "sourceMap": true, "moduleResolution": "node", "lib": ["es6"] }, "exclude": ["node_modules"] }

      现在我们可以开始创建我们的包了，为此让我们进入packages目录并首先创建common包。

       npm create vite@latest common --template react-ts

      我们现在应该在packages目录中有一个common包，现在让我们进入common目录并运行npm install来安装所有必需的依赖项。

       # npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common npm install

      为了使用我们在根级别定义的基本 Typescript 配置，我们需要在 common 包内的tsconfig.json的开头添加以下代码片段。

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/tsconfig.json { "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", ... }

      我们现在将继续创建一个 Button 组件并将其导出，以便我们的 monorepo 中的其他包可以使用它。

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/src/components/Button.tsx interface ButtonProps { buttonText: string; } const Button = ({ buttonText }: ButtonProps) => { return ( <button>{buttonText}</button> ) } export default Button;

      现在从main.tsx文件导出这个 Button 组件，这将是我们的入口点。

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/src/main.tsx import Button from "./components/Button" export { Button }

      我们将通过在package.json中设置main属性来将main.tsx设置为我们的入口点

      此外，根据 monorepos 的通用工作区命名约定，我们将common包的名称更改为@npm-lerna-monorepo/common

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/package.json { "name": "@npm-lerna-monorepo/common", // Change the package name "private": true, "version": "0.0.0", "type": "module", "main": "./src/main.tsx", // Define the entrypoint file ... }

      在将它与client包链接后，我们现在应该能够很容易地从common包导入组件。

      现在让我们创建我们的client包，为此我们将移动到包目录中并运行以下命令：

       # npm-lerna-monorepo/packages npm create vite@latest client --template react-ts

      运行上述命令后，我们的项目结构应如下所示：

      现在让我们更新客户端包的tsconfig.json以扩展我们在根级别定义的基本 Typescript 配置。

      为此，请在client/tsconfig.json的开头添加以下代码行：

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client/tsconfig.json { "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", ... }

      现在添加common包作为client包的依赖项之一，我们可以使用我们在其package.json文件中定义的名称添加公共包。

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client/package.json { "name": "@npm-lerna-monorepo/client", // Updated the name ... "dependencies": { "react": "^18.2.0", "react-dom": "^18.2.0", "@npm-lerna-monorepo/common": "*" // Added common package ... },

      我们还根据 monorepos 工作区命名约定更改了client包的名称。

      让我们通过从根级别运行npm install将我们的两个包链接在一起。

       # npm-lerna-monorepo npm install

      这会将两个包链接在一起，现在我们可以轻松地从common包中导入 Button 组件并在我们的client包中使用它。

       // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client/src/App.tsx import { Button } from "@npm-lerna-monorepo/common" const App = () => { return <Button buttonText="Hello world" /> } export default App;

      现在为client端包运行开发服务器。

       # npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client npm run dev

      在您最喜欢的 Web 浏览器上打开localhost:5173 ，您应该会在 UI 上看到一个按钮。

      因此，我们已经使用 Npm 工作区和 Lerna 成功设置了我们的 monorepo，当您在我们的本地环境中时，您对common包所做的任何更改都会立即反映在client上。

      万岁🎉

      您可以在此处找到设置的完整代码。

      结论

      在本文中，我们了解了如何使用 Lerna 和 Npm 工作区设置和管理一个单一仓库。

      虽然 Lerna 是一个很棒的工具，但你不需要将它包含在你的所有 monorepos 中，它主要适用于你有多个需要同时管理的包的大型应用程序。

      虽然本文主要关注设置部分，但如果您有兴趣阅读更多关于我们如何使用 Lerna 构建和发布我们的 monorepos 的信息，请在评论中告诉我。

      我希望这个博客能对你有点价值。 如果您喜欢它，请与可能觉得它有帮助的人分享，并在文章中展示一些内容。

      您还可以在Twitter上关注我，我会在其中发布更多关于 Javascript、React、软件工程和其他内容的内容。

      一旦暂停，imvedanshmehra 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

      解除暂停后，imvedanshmehra 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

      一旦取消发布，imvedanshmehra 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

      如果 imvedanshmehra 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

      笔记：

      感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 imvedanshmehra：

      降低 imvedanshmehra 的所有帖子的可见度

      报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 imvedanshmehra 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Monorepos lets you manage multiple repositories into one single repository but managing and publishing them can be very tedious as the repositories grow.

    Lerna is a powerful tool that can be used to manage and publish monorepos in Javascript/Typescript projects.

    Lerna is a fast, modern build system for managing and publishing multiple JavaScript/TypeScript packages from the same repository.

    In this article, we will see how we can set up a monorepo with Lerna and Npm workspaces.

    If you are interested in setting up a monorepo with Vite, Pnpm, and Typescript you can check out my previous blog here.

    Why use Lerna?

    To use monorepos well, you need a good monorepo tool, and Lerna is exactly that. Lerna is fast, widely used, and battle tested.

    Monorepos can have tens if not hundreds of packages, and managing all of them together can become very difficult. Lerna can rescue us in this situation by doing the heavy lifting of managing the tasks in different packages, versioning, code deployment, etc.

    Lerna has some great in-built features which make managing monorepos surprisingly easier.

    • Running tasks within multiple packages : Lerna gives us an out-of-the-box solution to run tasks inside multiple packages together using lerna run command. For example, Let's say you have 3 packages inside your monorepo and you want to run the test script for all of them, with Lerna all you have to do is just run npx lerna run test command from the root of your project and it will run the test script inside each of your packages (You must have defined the test script in package.json for all the projects respectively in order to make this work properly).

    • Visualize the workspace : When the workspace becomes very huge it is common for a new developer to face difficulty in understanding the hierarchy of the workspace and understand how different packages are linked together. Lerna uses Nx to help visualize the workspace graph. Simply run npx nx graph and it will launch a project visualization graph on your browser.

    You can check the complete list of Lerna features here.

    Who uses Lerna?

    Lerna is being used by many different teams including but not limited to:

    • Jest

    • Create React App

    • Nx

    • NestJS

    Project Setup

    Prerequisites

    To follow along, you must have Node installed in your system which I am assuming that you would already have. Other than that, some basic knowledge of Javascript/Typescript and React will help in understanding the code that we are going to write.

    Project Overview

    For the sake of this article, our monorepo will have two packages:

    1. common : A shared library that can be used by multiple packages.

    2. client : A basic React app that will consume the common package and will render the components on UI.

    Getting Started

    Let's start off by creating a new directory at your desired location.

    Then, move inside your newly created directory and initialize a Lerna workspace.

    # npm-lerna-monorepo
npx lerna init

    This command will do the following things:

    • Create a package.json file if it is not already present and add lerna as a dev dependency.

    • Create and configure a new lerna.json file.

    • Configure npm workspaces inside package.json.

    • Create a packages directory that will contain all our monorepo packages.

     lerna.json
 .gitignore
 package.json
 packages

    Now, in order to use Typescript in our project, we need to install a few dependencies at the root level.

    npm i typescript @types/node -D

    Once the above dependencies are installed, create a common tsconfig.json file that will be extended by all the other packages of our app.

    {
  "compilerOptions": {
    "module": "commonjs",
    "declaration": false,
    "noImplicitAny": false,
    "removeComments": true,
    "noLib": false,
    "emitDecoratorMetadata": true,
    "experimentalDecorators": true,
    "target": "es6",
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "moduleResolution": "node",
    "lib": ["es6"]
  },
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}

    Now we can start creating our packages, to do that let's go inside the packages directory and create the common package first.

    npm create vite@latest common --template react-ts

    We should now have a common package inside our packages directory, now let's move inside the common directory and run npm install to install all the required dependencies.

    # npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common
npm install

    In order to use the base Typescript configuration that we've defined at the root level, we need to add the following snippet at the beginning of our tsconfig.json inside the common package.

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/tsconfig.json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  ...
}

    We will now go ahead and create a Button component and will export it so that it can be used by the other packages in our monorepo.

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/src/components/Button.tsx

interface ButtonProps {
    buttonText: string;
}

const Button = ({ buttonText }: ButtonProps) => {
    return (
        <button>{buttonText}</button>
    )
}

export default Button;

    Now export this Button component from main.tsx file which will be our entry point.

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/src/main.tsx
import Button from "./components/Button"

export { Button }

    We will set main.tsx as our entry point by setting the main property in package.json

    Also, as per the common workspace naming convention of monorepos we'll change the name of the common package to @npm-lerna-monorepo/common

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/common/package.json
{
  "name": "@npm-lerna-monorepo/common", // Change the package name
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./src/main.tsx", // Define the entrypoint file
  ...
}

    We should now be able to import components from the common package very easily after linking it with the client package.

    Let's now create our client package, for that we'll move inside the packages directory and run the following command:

    # npm-lerna-monorepo/packages
npm create vite@latest client --template react-ts

    Our project structure after running the above command should look somewhat like this:

    Let's update the tsconfig.json of the client package now to extend the base Typescript configuration that we've defined at the root level.

    To do that add the following line of code at the beginning of client/tsconfig.json :

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client/tsconfig.json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  ...
}

    Now add the common package as one of the dependencies for the client package, we can add the common package using the name that we've defined in its package.json file.

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client/package.json
{
  "name": "@npm-lerna-monorepo/client", // Updated the name
  ...
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^18.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.2.0",
    "@npm-lerna-monorepo/common": "*" // Added common package
    ...
  },

    We've also changed the name of the client package according to the monorepos workspace naming convention.

    Let's link both of our packages together by running npm install from the root level.

    # npm-lerna-monorepo
npm install

    This will link both packages together and now we can easily import the Button component from the common package and use it in our client package.

    // npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client/src/App.tsx
import { Button } from "@npm-lerna-monorepo/common"

const App = () => {
  return <Button buttonText="Hello world" />
}

export default App;

    Run the dev server for client package now.

    # npm-lerna-monorepo/packages/client
npm run dev

    Open localhost:5173 on your favorite web browser and you should see a button rendered on the UI.

    So we have successfully setup up our monorepo using Npm workspaces and Lerna and while you are in our local environment, any changes you will make to the common package will immediately reflect on the client.

    Hooray 🎉

    You can find the complete code of the setup here.

    Conclusion

    In this article, we have seen how we can set up and manage a monorepo with Lerna and Npm workspaces.

    Though Lerna is a great tool, you don't need to include it in all of your monorepos it's mainly suitable for large applications where you have multiple packages that you need to manage simultaneously.

    While this article was majorly focused on the setup part, if you are interested in reading more about how we can use Lerna to build and publish our monorepos please let me know in the comments.

    I hope this blog could a little value to you. If you liked it please share it with those who might find it helpful and show some to the article.

    You can also follow me on Twitter where I post more content on Javascript, React, Software Engineering, and other stuff.

    Once suspended, imvedanshmehra will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, imvedanshmehra will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by imvedanshmehra will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If imvedanshmehra is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag imvedanshmehra:

    Make all posts by imvedanshmehra less visible

    imvedanshmehra consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging imvedanshmehra will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: imvedanshmehra
